reason: Ready for review: trace.* schema migration + ownership/idempotence/append-only-shape tests, all green (10 trace + 6 schema/store = 16 passed)

review.reference: Review passed: 005_trace_audit.sql matches existing migration style (CREATE...IF NOT EXISTS, loaded via duck.run_migrations lexical order); all 4 trace.* tables (research_session/tool_call/tool_result/surfaced_mark) under trace.* only, zero hp.* DDL (3 hp. refs are comments); stable VARCHAR PKs, FKs attach results/marks to immutable call_id, indexes on session_id, (session_id,hypothesis_identity), and call_id; no disclosure/aggregate cache table; tests use public duck.initialize fixture (no migration internals), assert schema/tables exist, hp.* survives, idempotence, and ownership guardrail. 16 passed (10 trace + 6 schema/store). Only the two owned files changed; no MCP/engine changes.

Ready for review: MCP trace tools (open/mark/disclosure) on default surface + opt-in analytical recording on change_point/smoothed_average/correlate + engine-purity regression, all green

review.reference: Review passed: 93/93 MCP tests green (test_mcp_trace_tools+recording+server+signal+analytical+correlate). LIVE wiring confirmed — all 3 analytical tools (change_point/smoothed_average/correlate) route through the sole _dispatch_analytical_with_trace helper which is the only caller of trace.start_recorded_call/finish_recorded_call; not dead code. Trace tools on default surface via _register_default_tools (operator inherits, delta == exactly query_warehouse). Purity regression is genuine (byte-identical engine envelope traced vs untraced, sort_keys json compare, trace key only at wrapper layer). Opt-in proven (no session_id => no row, no trace key). Refusal/retry/not-found/exclusion all tested through MCP boundary. 4 out-of-scope count-test edits are purely additive (3 trace tool names) — no assertion weakened. Boundary fixes (bracketed writable conns; test-only warehouse.duckdb rename) are sound; product schema still trace.*.
